summary: Gruber reviews the 2018 iPad Pros, marveling at their A12X chip performance that now rivals the fastest MacBook Pros at a fraction of the cost. He praises the redesigned Apple Pencil 2.0 as nearing perfection, highlights the new edge-to-edge display and flat-sided industrial design, and notes Face ID works well in all orientations. While acknowledging the high total cost with accessories, he argues these are serious computing devices that deliver better value dollar-for-dollar than any MacBook. He still personally prefers a Mac for portable work but recognizes the iPad Pro's legitimacy as a primary computer for many users.
key_points: The A12X chip makes the iPad Pro competitive in both single- and multi-core performance with the top-end MacBook Pro, at a fraction of the price
The Apple Pencil 2.0 is a near-perfect revision: magnetic charging, matte finish, no cap to lose, flat side to prevent rolling, and double-tap gestures
The new industrial design with flat sides and edge-to-edge display is the best-looking iOS device Apple has made, and Gruber wants iPhones to adopt this look
iOS still has significant gaps for a 'pro' device: the Files app doesn't show USB drives even with a USB-C port, and Face ID only supports two faces despite iPads being shared family devices
Dollar for dollar, these iPad Pros deliver better value than any MacBook Apple has ever made
money_quotes: The iPad Pro is like a computer from an alternate universe. In the normal universe, Moore's Law has stopped delivering significant year-over-year returns, and high-performance portables need fans to cool them. In the iPad universe, Moore's Law still delivers year after year.
Its performance, both from the CPU and GPU, is simply bananas. It's nuts. Astounding performance per dollar, astounding performance per watt.

Apple bragged during the iPad Pro introduction that they are faster than 92 percent of notebook PCs sold in the last year. That's not so funny when you consider that 'PCs', in this formulation, includes MacBooks.
The new Apple Pencil is one of the best '2.0' products I've ever seen.
It doesn't make sense to me to have a Files app but not show the contents of USB drives when you connect them. This just feels spiteful.
I think this is not just the best-looking iPad ever, but the best looking iOS device ever.
Dollar for dollar, they're a better value than any MacBook Apple has ever made.
These are serious iPads for serious iPad users.
key_insight: The 2018 iPad Pros represent a inflection point where tablet performance matches top-end laptops at a fraction of the cost, making them genuinely competitive as primary computers rather than just companion devices.
